item.title: index
item.description: Hello, Hola! This is more than just a blog, it’s a workshop for the curious and a log of my journey through the ever-evolving world of software engineering.

item.title: n8n is pure awesomeness
item.description: One of the big benefits of having a Homelab and plenty of services running locally is that I can start connecting them together and using them for various things.

item.title: Are you stuck?
item.description: You’ve probably heard people in the IT space talk about being “burned out” and what leads to it.

item.title: My First Workshop
item.description: Part of my resolutions for this year is to do more public speaking so I’ve been looking for oportunities to do so at local meetups, company conferences or anything basically.

item.title: Yaml is King change my mind, please!
item.description: Got a short one for you this week.

item.title: Bye Elastic, Hola Victoria
item.description: It is no secret that I’m a Elasticsearch aficionado, try to use it for a lot of things like storing my twitch chat messages or visualize data (you can do a lookup for elastic on this blog and find 3-4 posts about it), hell I even get paid to manage couple clusters at work and it’s been pretty close ...

item.title: You shouldn't use Neovim
item.description: I know this will sound weird specially coming from someone that lives and spends 70% of my time living in a terminal and using neovim btw but sometimes it’s really ok to not use the hot and cool tool that people tell you to use.

item.title: Golang Interfaces are easy
item.description: Recently been doing a lot of Interfaces for my twitch bot (I stream btw, rarely but I do).

item.title: Golang Interfaces are easy
item.description: Recently been doing a lot of Golang for my twitch bot (I stream btw, rarely but I do).

item.title: Obsidian Like Blog
item.description: After a year of immersing myself in the world of Obsidian, I’ve grown accustomed to using it for everything from notes and posts to task tracking and work documentation.
